title: Immigrate to a Francophone community outside Quebec - Canada.ca
content:
## Immigrate to a Francophone community outside Quebec
Are you an employer? Hire qualified French-speaking and bilingual candidates .

## Plan your life in Canada
- Get Canadian work experience The Francophone Mobility work permit makes it easier for Canadian employers to hire you.
Get Canadian work experience
The Francophone Mobility work permit makes it easier for Canadian employers to hire you.
- Come to Canada as a skilled worker through Express Entry Immigrate to Canada as a skilled worker. Get extra Express Entry points for your French and English language skills.
Come to Canada as a skilled worker through Express Entry
Immigrate to Canada as a skilled worker. Get extra Express Entry points for your French and English language skills.
- Work and travel in Canada as a youth If you’re between 18 and 35, you may be eligible for International Experience Canada. We’re partners with over 35 countries or territories, including France and Belgium.
Work and travel in Canada as a youth
If you’re between 18 and 35, you may be eligible for International Experience Canada. We’re partners with over 35 countries or territories, including France and Belgium.
- Find a job through the Destination Canada Mobility Forum Find employers and job opportunities. Discover Francophone communities outside Quebec.
Find a job through the Destination Canada Mobility Forum
Find employers and job opportunities. Discover Francophone communities outside Quebec.
- Study as a French-speaking student If you want to study in French in Canada and you’re from a specific country, you may be eligible to apply under the Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot.
Study as a French-speaking student
If you want to study in French in Canada and you’re from a specific country, you may be eligible to apply under the Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot.
- Get hired in a Francophone community The Francophone Community Immigration Pilot offers permanent residence to skilled workers who want to work and settle in rural and more remote Francophone communities.
Get hired in a Francophone community
The Francophone Community Immigration Pilot offers permanent residence to skilled workers who want to work and settle in rural and more remote Francophone communities.

## Preparing to leave for Canada
- Living in French Learn about the benefits of living in a Francophone community outside Quebec.
Learn about the benefits of living in a Francophone community outside Quebec.
- Choose a welcoming community that offers services in French 24 communities have been selected to welcome and support your life in Canada. Discover each community’s unique art, culture and environment.
Choose a welcoming community that offers services in French
24 communities have been selected to welcome and support your life in Canada. Discover each community’s unique art, culture and environment.
- Services to get you ready for life in Canada Before coming to Canada, get help finding a job and preparing for your financial, social and cultural life in Canada.
Services to get you ready for life in Canada
Before coming to Canada, get help finding a job and preparing for your financial, social and cultural life in Canada.

## Access services in French after you arrive in Canada
- Newcomer services Get help in French with your job search, health care, language skills and more.
Get help in French with your job search, health care, language skills and more.
- Watch these videos on Francophone immigration to Canada
Watch these videos on Francophone immigration to Canada

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
